name: 3-3-1 model (SU(3)_C x SU(3)_L x U(1)_X)
summary: Extends the SM weak-isospin SU(2)_L to SU(3)_L, with a TeV-scale U(1)_X factor, producing an electroweak gauge group SU(3)_C x SU(3)_L x U(1)_X that breaks to SM near the TeV scale. Fermions are arranged in triplets/anti-triplets; anomaly cancellation requires exactly three generations (proportional to colour). Provides a 'why three families' answer at the cost of a richer Higgs and gauge sector.

addresses_puzzles.note: Anomaly cancellation in SU(3)_L x U(1)_X is non-trivial: each lepton generation contributes a triangle anomaly that is cancelled only by an integer number of quark generations equal to the number of colours (3). Forces N_gen = 3 exactly. Among the few known mechanisms that derive 3 from anomaly consistency.
Embeds chiral structure naturally; the SU(3)_L assignment is forced by anomaly cancellation.

touches_benchmarks.note: TeV-scale Z' and exotic doubly-charged gauge bosons contribute to S, T at observable levels; current EW precision pushes M_Z' > ~3-4 TeV.
Z' tree-level FCNCs from non-universal flavour assignments; K-Kbar and B-Bbar bounds constrain TeV-scale 331 implementations.
Doubly-charged scalar and exotic-quark loops contribute to (g-2)_mu; 331 model can fit FNAL+BNL anomaly in part of parameter space.

new_fields: vector-like exotic quarks T, D, S
introduces.note: Gauge couplings, Higgs sector parameters, exotic Yukawa structure, breaking scale.

history: Pisano and Pleitez (1992), 'SU(3) x U(1) model for electroweak interactions', Phys. Rev. D 46, 410. Frampton (1992, PRL 69, 2889) independently. Active variant model-building 2010s-2020s especially in connection with B-anomalies and (g-2)_mu.

references.citation: Pisano and Pleitez, 'SU(3) x U(1) model for electroweak interactions', Phys. Rev. D 46, 410 (1992)
Frampton, 'Chiral dilepton model and the flavor question', Phys. Rev. Lett. 69, 2889 (1992)
